## Coding Practices {#coding-practices}

Good coding practices keep PEcAn's codebase clean, readable, and maintainable. This section covers the standards and conventions all contributors should follow.

Much of the advice in this section is not specific to PEcAn and is instead summarizing R community norms for good practice in package development. We drew especially heavily from the following sources, all of which are worth reading and following.

* [Writing R Extensions](https://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/r-release/R-exts.html) is maintained by the R Core Team and forms the definitive source on how R packages should and should not behave.
* [R Packages](https://r-pkgs.org) presents an opinionated but practical and detailed guide to one successful approach to R package development.
* The [Tidyverse style guide](https://style.tidyverse.org) defines the code style conventions that PEcAn has adopted as its [aspirational] house style.
